  • MotherBoard Beep Code

Motherboard Beep Code Reference

Diagnose hardware failures using POST (Power-On Self Test) audio signals.

What Are BIOS Beep Codes?

When you turn on a computer, the motherboard performs POST (Power-On Self Test) to check essential hardware. If there is a fault before video output is available, the motherboard can emit a series of beep patterns through the speaker.

These sounds follow known patterns and can quickly point to RAM, CPU, GPU, motherboard, or BIOS-related problems.

Important: Beep codes are often the first and only diagnostics when there is no screen output.

Beep Code Reference Tables

Select your BIOS manufacturer. If unsure, check your motherboard manual or startup splash screen.

AMI (American Megatrends Inc.) BIOS Beep Codes

Common in many desktop and server motherboards. BIOS version may slightly change the meaning.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory Read/Write test failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register read/write error Motherboard Del / F2
11 short beeps Cache memory error RAM Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card memory issue GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short Conventional/Extended memory failure RAM Del / F2
1 long, 8 short Display/Retrace test failed GPU Del / F2
Two-tone siren Low CPU fan speed or voltage level issue CPU Del / F2
Award BIOS Beep Codes

Award BIOS (now part of Phoenix) is common in older motherboards. Most errors display a message if a screen is available.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 long, 2 short Video error â€” BIOS cannot initialize the video screen GPU Del
1 long, 3 short Video card not detected or bad video card GPU Del
Repeating endlessly RAM problem RAM Del
Repeated high frequency Overheating processor CPU Del
Alternating high & low Processor issue, possibly damaged CPU Del
Dell BIOS Beep Codes

Applies to Dell desktops and laptops. Codes are consistent across most Dell models.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep BIOS ROM corruption or failure BIOS F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
2 beeps Memory (RAM) not detected RAM F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
3 beeps Motherboard failure Motherboard F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
4 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
5 beeps CMOS battery failure Motherboard F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
6 beeps Video card failure GPU F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
7 beeps Bad processor (CPU) CPU F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
IBM BIOS Beep Codes

General IBM BIOS codes. Varies across different IBM models and generations.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
No beeps No power or component not turning on Motherboard F1 / F2
1 short beep Normal POST â€” computer is OK OK F1 / F2
2 short beeps POST error â€” review screen for error code Motherboard F1 / F2
Continuous beep No power, loose card, or short circuit Motherboard F1 / F2
Repeating short beep No power, loose card, or short circuit Motherboard F1 / F2
1 long, 1 short Motherboard issue Motherboard F1 / F2
1 long, 2 short Video (Mono/CGA display circuitry) issue GPU F1 / F2
1 long, 3 short Video (EGA) display circuitry GPU F1 / F2
3 long beeps Keyboard or keyboard card error Keyboard F1 / F2
1 beep + blank display Video display circuitry GPU F1 / F2
Phoenix BIOS Beep Codes

Phoenix uses a pattern of groups separated by pauses (e.g., 1-3-1-1 means 1 beep, pause, 3 beeps, pause, 1 beep, pause, 1 beep). Only the most common codes are listed.

Beep Pattern Description Component BIOS Key
1-1-1-1 Unconfirmed â€” reseat or replace RAM chips RAM F2 / Esc
1-3-1-1 Test DRAM refresh RAM F2 / Esc
1-3-3-1 Autosize DRAM failure RAM F2 / Esc
2-1-2-1 Initialize BIOS interrupts BIOS F2 / Esc
2-1-3-2 Initialize PCI bus and devices Motherboard F2 / Esc
2-2-3-1 Test for unexpected interrupts CPU F2 / Esc
3-1-1-1 Disable onboard I/O ports Motherboard F2 / Esc
3-2-1-1 Initialize hard disk controller Motherboard F2 / Esc
4-2-1-1 Interrupt handler error CPU F2 / Esc
4-3-2-4 Complete RAM test failure RAM F2 / Esc
Two-tone siren Low CPU fan speed or voltage issue CPU F2 / Esc
HP / Compaq BIOS Beep Codes

Used in HP and Compaq desktops and laptops. Codes may vary slightly between models and generations.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F10 / Esc
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard F10 / Esc
1 long, 1 short BIOS ROM checksum error BIOS F10 / Esc
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F10 / Esc
1 long, 3 short No video card or bad video RAM GPU F10 / Esc
2 short, 1 long RAM parity error in first 64K RAM F10 / Esc
3 long beeps Keyboard controller error Motherboard F10 / Esc
4 beeps System timer failure Motherboard F10 / Esc
5 beeps Processor (CPU) failure CPU F10 / Esc
6 beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 failure Motherboard F10 / Esc
7 beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU F10 / Esc
8 beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU F10 / Esc
9 beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS F10 / Esc
Continuous beeps Memory or video problem RAM F10 / Esc
Lenovo BIOS Beep Codes

Applies to Lenovo ThinkPad and ThinkCentre series. Some models use LED blink codes alongside beeps.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
2 short beeps Initialization error â€” check screen Motherboard F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
1 long, 1 short System board (motherboard) error Motherboard F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
1 long, 3 short EGA/VGA video error GPU F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
1 long, 4 short Serial or parallel port error Motherboard F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
2 long, 1 short RAM/DRAM error RAM F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
2 long, 3 short No display adapter or video error GPU F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
2 long, 4 short Video adapter memory error GPU F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
3 short beeps DRAM error â€” memory module failure RAM F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
Continuous beeps Thermal shutdown â€” overheating CPU F1 / F2 (Fn+F2)
ASUS BIOS Beep Codes

ASUS motherboards typically use AMI BIOS. Codes below apply to most ASUS desktop boards. Newer UEFI models may use Q-LEDs instead.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Acer BIOS Beep Codes

Acer desktops and laptops use Phoenix or AMI BIOS depending on model. The codes below are for common Acer systems.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Del
2 beeps Video adapter error or not found GPU F2 / Del
3 beeps Keyboard not detected Motherboard F2 / Del
4 beeps RAM error â€” not detected or failure RAM F2 / Del
5 beeps RTC (Real-Time Clock) error Motherboard F2 / Del
6 beeps BIOS image checksum error BIOS F2 / Del
1 long, 1 short System board failure Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video card failure GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Memory error RAM F2 / Del
High-low repeating CPU overheating or fan failure CPU F2 / Del
Continuous beeps Power supply or motherboard failure Motherboard F2 / Del
MSI BIOS Beep Codes

MSI motherboards use AMI BIOS. Codes match standard AMI patterns across most MSI desktop and server boards.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not seated â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
Gigabyte BIOS Beep Codes

Gigabyte boards use Award or AMI BIOS depending on the generation. Newer UEFI boards may emit different tones.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” no error OK Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card failure or not detected GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No keyboard detected Motherboard Del / F2
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard Del / F2
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Repeated short beeps Power issue or short circuit Motherboard Del / F2
Repeated high frequency CPU overheating CPU Del / F2
Apple / Mac Beep Codes

Apple uses tones instead of beeps. Newer Macs may use LED blink patterns alongside tones. Applies to Intel-based Macs.

Beep / Tone Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 tone No RAM installed or not detected RAM Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
2 tones Incompatible RAM types detected RAM Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
3 tones No RAM banks passed memory testing RAM Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
4 tones Bad checksum for boot ROM remainder BIOS Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
5 tones ROM cannot properly communicate with RAM RAM Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
6 tones Boot ROM does not support the sleep image BIOS Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
3 long, 3 short, 3 long EFI ROM corruption â€” update required BIOS Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
1 long tone (hold power) EFI ROM corruption found â€” recovery mode BIOS Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
Tones repeat 3Ã— RAM failure â€” reseat or replace DIMMs RAM Option ⌥ (Startup Manager)
Intel Desktop Board BIOS Beep Codes

Applies to Intel brand desktop motherboards. Uses AMI BIOS. Consumer Intel boards have been discontinued but are still widely in use.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep BIOS ROM failure BIOS F2 / Del
2 beeps Processor failure CPU F2 / Del
3 beeps Memory error (first or third 64KB block) RAM F2 / Del
4 beeps Timer not operational Motherboard F2 / Del
5 beeps Processor error CPU F2 / Del
6 beeps Gate A20 cannot be toggled (8042 error) Motherboard F2 / Del
7 beeps Processor exception interrupt error CPU F2 / Del
8 beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU F2 / Del
9 beeps ROM checksum error BIOS F2 / Del
10 beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard F2 / Del
11 beeps Cache memory error RAM F2 / Del
3 continuous long beeps Memory (RAM) error â€” reseat or replace RAM F2 / Del
Toshiba BIOS Beep Codes

Applies to Toshiba laptops and desktops. Uses Phoenix or AMI BIOS. Some models combine blink and beep codes.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Esc
2 beeps POST failure â€” check screen for details Motherboard F2 / Esc
1 long, 1 short System board (motherboard) error Motherboard F2 / Esc
1 long, 2 short Video adapter failure GPU F2 / Esc
1 long, 3 short No keyboard detected Motherboard F2 / Esc
3 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2 / Esc
Repeating beeps RAM issue â€” reseat or replace module RAM F2 / Esc
Continuous beeps Thermal shutdown â€” overheating CPU F2 / Esc
ASRock BIOS Beep Codes

ASRock boards use AMI UEFI BIOS. Standard AMI beep codes apply. Newer models may also use Dr. Debug LED display.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Gateway / eMachines BIOS Beep Codes

Gateway and eMachines desktops typically use AMI or Phoenix BIOS. Codes below apply to common Gateway models.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Del
2 beeps POST failure â€” check screen for code Motherboard F2 / Del
3 beeps Keyboard controller error Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Video card not detected GPU F2 / Del
Continuous beeps RAM failure â€” reseat or replace RAM F2 / Del
Repeated short beeps Power issue or loose component Motherboard F2 / Del
Sony VAIO BIOS Beep Codes

Sony VAIO laptops use AMI or Phoenix BIOS depending on the model. Some models use blink codes instead of beeps.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Assist Button
2 beeps RAM failure â€” not detected or unseated RAM F2 / Assist Button
3 beeps Motherboard / system board error Motherboard F2 / Assist Button
1 long, 2 short Video card or display adapter failure GPU F2 / Assist Button
Continuous beeps Overheating or power issue CPU F2 / Assist Button
Repeating 2-beep pattern RAM error â€” reseat or replace module RAM F2 / Assist Button
No beep + no display Possible GPU or motherboard failure GPU F2 / Assist Button
Samsung BIOS Beep Codes

Samsung desktops and laptops use AMI BIOS. Standard AMI codes apply. Some models also use LED blink diagnostic codes.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2
2 beeps POST error â€” check screen for details Motherboard F2
3 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2
4 beeps System timer or motherboard failure Motherboard F2
5 beeps Processor (CPU) failure CPU F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU F2
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM F2
Repeated high-pitch beeps CPU overheating or fan failure CPU F2
Biostar BIOS Beep Codes

Biostar motherboards use AMI BIOS. Standard AMI beep codes apply across all Biostar desktop boards.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register read/write error Motherboard Del
11 short beeps Cache memory error RAM Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
ECS (Elitegroup Computer Systems) BIOS Beep Codes

ECS motherboards use AMI or Award BIOS. Commonly found in OEM builds and budget desktops.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” no error (Award) or DRAM refresh failure (AMI) OK / RAM Del
1 long, 2 short Video card failure or not detected GPU Del
1 long, 3 short Video card not found GPU Del
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
Repeating endlessly RAM problem RAM Del
Alternating high & low Processor issue, possibly damaged CPU Del
Foxconn BIOS Beep Codes

Foxconn retail motherboards use AMI BIOS. Foxconn also manufactures boards for other OEMs under different BIOS flavors.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
Zotac BIOS Beep Codes

Zotac mini-PCs and desktop boards use AMI UEFI BIOS. Standard AMI codes apply. Many Zotac systems are fanless/compact.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Supermicro BIOS Beep Codes

Supermicro server and workstation boards use AMI BIOS. These codes are widely used in rack-mount and blade server environments.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode CPU exception CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum error BIOS Del / F2
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del / F2
11 short beeps Cache memory BAD RAM Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card memory error GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short Conventional/Extended memory failure RAM Del / F2
1 long, 8 short Display / retrace test failed GPU Del / F2
Continuous beeps System memory failure â€” check DIMMs and slots RAM Del / F2
Fujitsu / Fujitsu-Siemens BIOS Beep Codes

Fujitsu desktops and laptops (including ESPRIMO and LIFEBOOK lines) use Phoenix or AMI BIOS depending on model generation.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F2
2 beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard F2
3 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2
4 beeps System timer failure Motherboard F2
5 beeps CPU failure CPU F2
1 long, 1 short System board failure Motherboard F2
1 long, 2 short Video card failure or not found GPU F2
1 long, 3 short No keyboard found Motherboard F2
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM F2
Repeating high-low Overheating â€” check cooling CPU F2
NEC BIOS Beep Codes

NEC desktops and laptops (including Versa and PowerMate lines) use Phoenix or AMI BIOS. Common in Japanese and enterprise markets.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2
2 beeps Video card or display adapter failure GPU F2
3 beeps Keyboard error or not found Motherboard F2
1 long, 1 short Motherboard failure Motherboard F2
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F2
1 long, 3 short Video (EGA) error GPU F2
Continuous beeps RAM failure â€” reseat or replace DIMMs RAM F2
Repeating short beeps Power supply or motherboard issue Motherboard F2
Packard Bell BIOS Beep Codes

Packard Bell desktops and laptops use Phoenix or AMI BIOS. Common in European markets. Now owned by Acer.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Del
2 beeps POST failure â€” check screen for code Motherboard F2 / Del
3 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2 / Del
1 long, 1 short System board error Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video card failure or not detected GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Keyboard or video error Motherboard F2 / Del
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM F2 / Del
Repeating high-low CPU overheating or fan failure CPU F2 / Del
Panasonic Toughbook BIOS Beep Codes

Panasonic Toughbook ruggedized laptops use Phoenix BIOS. Some models combine beep codes with LED indicators for field diagnostics.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F2 / Del
2 beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 1 short System board error Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video adapter failure GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Memory test failure RAM F2 / Del
3 beeps Keyboard failure or not detected Motherboard F2 / Del
Continuous beeps RAM failure â€” module not detected RAM F2 / Del
Repeating beeps Overheating â€” check thermal system CPU F2 / Del
Olivetti BIOS Beep Codes

Olivetti desktops and laptops use Phoenix BIOS. Primarily found in European enterprise and education environments.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Del
1 long, 1 short System board failure Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Video (EGA) display circuitry error GPU F2 / Del
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard F2 / Del
Continuous beep No power, loose card or short circuit Motherboard F2 / Del
Repeating short beep No power, loose card or short circuit Motherboard F2 / Del
3 long beeps Keyboard error or not detected Motherboard F2 / Del
Compaq BIOS Beep Codes (Pre-HP)

Original Compaq desktops and laptops (before HP acquisition) used a proprietary BIOS. Codes differ from the HP/Compaq combined line.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep No error â€” system booting normally OK F10
1 long, 1 short BIOS ROM failure BIOS F10
1 long, 2 short Video failure (Mono/CGA) GPU F10
1 long, 3 short Video failure (EGA/VGA) GPU F10
2 short beeps POST error â€” review screen message Motherboard F10
Continuous beep No power, loose card or short circuit Motherboard F10
Repeating short beep No power, loose card or short circuit Motherboard F10
3 long beeps Keyboard or keyboard controller error Motherboard F10
1 beep + blank display Video display circuitry failure GPU F10
Tandy BIOS Beep Codes

Tandy 1000 series PCs used a proprietary BIOS based on IBM XT/AT spec. A collector's reference for legacy hardware diagnostics.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK N/A (Legacy)
2 short beeps POST error â€” review screen for code Motherboard N/A (Legacy)
Continuous beep No power or loose card Motherboard N/A (Legacy)
Repeating short beep No power or short circuit Motherboard N/A (Legacy)
1 long, 1 short System board failure Motherboard N/A (Legacy)
1 long, 2 short Video (Mono/CGA) display circuitry issue GPU N/A (Legacy)
1 long, 3 short Video (EGA) display circuitry issue GPU N/A (Legacy)
3 long beeps Keyboard or keyboard card error Motherboard N/A (Legacy)
1 beep + blank screen Video display failure GPU N/A (Legacy)
Alienware BIOS Beep Codes

Alienware gaming laptops and desktops are owned by Dell and use Dell's BIOS. Beep codes are identical to standard Dell codes across all Alienware models (m15, m17, x15, x17, Area-51m, Aurora, etc.).

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep BIOS ROM corruption or failure BIOS F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
2 beeps Memory (RAM) not detected RAM F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
3 beeps Motherboard failure Motherboard F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
4 beeps Memory (RAM) failure RAM F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
5 beeps CMOS battery failure Motherboard F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
6 beeps Video card failure GPU F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
7 beeps Bad processor (CPU) CPU F2 (Setup) / F12 (Boot)
ASUS ROG / TUF Gaming BIOS Beep Codes

ASUS ROG (Republic of Gamers) and TUF Gaming laptops and desktops use AMI UEFI BIOS â€” identical to standard ASUS beep codes. Applies to ROG Zephyrus, Strix, Flow, Scar, and TUF Gaming A/F series.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Lenovo Legion Gaming BIOS Beep Codes

Lenovo Legion gaming laptops (Legion 5, 5i, 5 Pro, 7, 7i, Slim 5/7) use the same BIOS beep codes as standard Lenovo ThinkPad/ThinkCentre, with some models additionally using LED blink indicators.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F2 / Fn+F2
2 short beeps Initialization error â€” check screen Motherboard F2 / Fn+F2
1 long, 1 short System board (motherboard) error Motherboard F2 / Fn+F2
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F2 / Fn+F2
1 long, 3 short EGA/VGA video error GPU F2 / Fn+F2
1 long, 4 short Serial or parallel port error Motherboard F2 / Fn+F2
2 long, 1 short RAM/DRAM error RAM F2 / Fn+F2
2 long, 3 short No display adapter or video error GPU F2 / Fn+F2
2 long, 4 short Video adapter memory error GPU F2 / Fn+F2
3 short beeps DRAM error â€” memory module failure RAM F2 / Fn+F2
Continuous beeps Thermal shutdown â€” overheating CPU F2 / Fn+F2
HP OMEN Gaming BIOS Beep Codes

HP OMEN gaming laptops and desktops use the same AMI/Phoenix BIOS beep codes as standard HP models. Applies to OMEN 15, 16, 17, OMEN Transcend, and OMEN desktop series.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” system is OK OK F10 / Esc
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard F10 / Esc
1 long, 1 short BIOS ROM checksum error BIOS F10 / Esc
1 long, 2 short Video adapter error GPU F10 / Esc
1 long, 3 short No video card or bad video RAM GPU F10 / Esc
2 short, 1 long RAM parity error in first 64K RAM F10 / Esc
3 long beeps Keyboard controller error Motherboard F10 / Esc
4 beeps System timer failure Motherboard F10 / Esc
5 beeps Processor (CPU) failure CPU F10 / Esc
6 beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 failure Motherboard F10 / Esc
7 beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU F10 / Esc
8 beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU F10 / Esc
9 beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS F10 / Esc
Continuous beeps Memory or video problem RAM F10 / Esc
Acer Predator / Nitro Gaming BIOS Beep Codes

Acer Predator and Nitro gaming laptops use the same Phoenix or AMI BIOS as standard Acer systems. Applies to Predator Helios, Triton, Nitro 5/7, and Predator Orion desktop series.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 beep Normal POST â€” no error OK F2 / Del
2 beeps Video adapter error or not found GPU F2 / Del
3 beeps Keyboard not detected Motherboard F2 / Del
4 beeps RAM error â€” not detected or failure RAM F2 / Del
5 beeps RTC (Real-Time Clock) error Motherboard F2 / Del
6 beeps BIOS image checksum error BIOS F2 / Del
1 long, 1 short System board failure Motherboard F2 / Del
1 long, 2 short Video card failure GPU F2 / Del
1 long, 3 short Memory error RAM F2 / Del
High-low repeating CPU overheating or fan failure CPU F2 / Del
Continuous beeps Power supply or motherboard failure Motherboard F2 / Del
Gigabyte AORUS Gaming BIOS Beep Codes

Gigabyte AORUS gaming laptops and motherboards use the same Award or AMI BIOS as standard Gigabyte boards. Applies to AORUS 15, 17, Master, Xtreme, and AORUS desktop motherboard series.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep Normal POST â€” no error OK Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card failure or not detected GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No keyboard detected Motherboard Del / F2
2 short beeps POST error â€” check screen for code Motherboard Del / F2
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Repeated short beeps Power issue or short circuit Motherboard Del / F2
Repeated high frequency CPU overheating CPU Del / F2
Razer Blade Gaming Laptop BIOS Beep Codes

Razer Blade laptops (Blade 14, 15, 16, 18, Blade Stealth) use AMI UEFI BIOS on custom Razer motherboards. Standard AMI beep codes apply.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
MSI Gaming Laptop BIOS Beep Codes

MSI gaming laptops (Stealth, Raider, Titan, Katana, Cyborg, Vector, Sword series) use AMI UEFI BIOS on MSI proprietary motherboards â€” same codes as MSI desktop boards.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not seated â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
EVGA Gaming Motherboard BIOS Beep Codes

EVGA made high-end gaming and enthusiast motherboards (Z270, Z370, Z390, Z490, Z590, Z690, X299 series) using AMI UEFI BIOS. EVGA exited the motherboard market in 2022 but boards remain widely in use.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del
11 short beeps Cache memory error RAM Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
1 long, 8 short Display/Retrace test failed GPU Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
Two-tone siren Low CPU fan speed or voltage level issue CPU Del
NZXT Gaming Motherboard BIOS Beep Codes

NZXT N7 and N5 series gaming motherboards (Intel Z790, Z690, B650, X570 platforms) use AMI UEFI BIOS. Standard AMI codes apply. NZXT boards are known for their minimalist design and CAM software integration.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
MSI MEG / MPG / MAG Gaming Motherboard BIOS Beep Codes

MSI gaming motherboard lines â€” MEG (enthusiast), MPG (performance), MAG (mainstream) â€” all use AMI UEFI BIOS. Applies to MEG Z790 ACE, MPG Z790 Carbon, MAG B760M Mortar, and equivalent AMD platform boards.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
10 short beeps CMOS shutdown register error Motherboard Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not seated â€” reseat or replace RAM Del
ASRock Phantom Gaming / Taichi Motherboard BIOS Beep Codes

ASRock's gaming and enthusiast motherboard lines â€” Phantom Gaming, Taichi, Steel Legend, and PG Riptide â€” use AMI UEFI BIOS. Same codes apply across all ASRock boards. Newer models may also feature Dr. Debug LED readout.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del / F2
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del / F2
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del / F2
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del / F2
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del / F2
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del / F2
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del / F2
8 short beeps Display memory failure GPU Del / F2
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del / F2
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del / F2
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del / F2
Continuous beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del / F2
Colorful / iGame Gaming Motherboard BIOS Beep Codes

Colorful and its gaming sub-brand iGame produce Intel and AMD motherboards popular in Asian markets (iGame Z790D5 Ultra, CVN B650M). All models use AMI UEFI BIOS and follow standard AMI beep codes.

Beep Code Description Component BIOS Key
1 short beep DRAM refresh failure RAM Del
2 short beeps Parity circuit failure RAM Del
3 short beeps Base 64K RAM failure RAM Del
4 short beeps System timer failure Motherboard Del
5 short beeps Processor failure CPU Del
6 short beeps Keyboard controller / Gate A20 error Motherboard Del
7 short beeps Virtual mode exception error CPU Del
8 short beeps Display memory read/write failure GPU Del
9 short beeps ROM BIOS checksum failure BIOS Del
1 long, 2 short Video card not detected or faulty GPU Del
1 long, 3 short No RAM installed or RAM failure RAM Del
Continuous long beeps RAM not detected â€” reseat or replace RAM Del

Troubleshooting Tips

01

Identify your BIOS

Check startup splash screen or motherboard documentation.

02

Count beeps carefully

Short and long beep patterns matter. Phoenix codes include grouped pauses.

03

Reseat hardware

For RAM and GPU errors, reseat components before replacing them.

04

Check power and cables

Loose power connectors can trigger POST failures.
